name: Equipment Rarity
description: A tiering system that groups equipment by scarcity, power budget, presentation, and upgrade potential.
design_notes: Equipment rarity links reward excitement to balance math. A useful rarity system defines rarity_tier, stat budget, drop_weight, upgrade ceiling, duplicate handling, and presentation rules without letting color alone carry the meaning.
design_purpose: Signal item scarcity and expected value quickly.
Control power budgets for loot, shops, crafting, and upgrade systems.
Create collection and build goals around rare item properties.
player_fantasy: The player feels the excitement of finding gear that is meaningfully special, not just differently colored.

parameters.name: rarity_tier
parameters.description: Canonical tier used for drop logic, UI, upgrade rules, and economy valuation.

parameters.name: stat_budget_multiplier
parameters.description: Multiplier applied to item stat budget or affix count for this tier.

parameters.name: drop_weight
parameters.description: Relative selection weight for this rarity in loot tables or vendors.

parameters.name: upgrade_level_cap
parameters.description: Maximum upgrade level allowed for equipment in this tier.

parameters.name: duplicate_rule
parameters.description: Handling for repeated or unneeded equipment of the same rarity and item family.

edge_cases: A low-level high-rarity item can be weaker than a later common item and confuse players if comparison is unclear.
Rarity tiers added in a patch need migration for old items and loot tables.
Duplicate unique items require a rule for ownership, conversion, or rerolling.
Crafting or upgrading can change rarity_tier and must preserve item_instance_id history or intentionally replace it.
common_bugs: Rarity color updates but stat_budget_multiplier does not, producing misleading items.
Loot tables filter by display rarity name instead of rarity_tier, breaking localization and balance tools.
Upgrade caps are checked in UI but not in the upgrade transaction.
Duplicate conversion grants currency but leaves the duplicate item in inventory.
balancing_notes: Rarity should change expected value, not guarantee that every item invalidates lower tiers immediately.
The rarest tier needs enough drop scarcity and upgrade cost to stay exciting without making ordinary rewards worthless.
If rarity controls both power and appearance, include cosmetic-only paths so players are not forced into ugly best-in-slot gear.
accessibility_notes: Communicate rarity with text labels and icons in addition to color.
Make item comparison readable with explicit stat changes, not only rarity names or glow effects.

implementation_notes.unity: Define rarity_tier rows in data and have loot, UI, and upgrade systems read the same stat_budget_multiplier and upgrade_level_cap rather than duplicating switch statements.
implementation_notes.godot: Store rarity definitions as Resources or JSON dictionaries keyed by rarity_tier; item instances should persist tier and generated affixes separately for migration.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Primary Data Assets or DataTables for rarity tiers and validate upgrade transactions on the authoritative inventory component using item_instance_id and tier cap.
implementation_notes.web: Keep item definitions and item_instance_id state separate; rarity display, drop generation, duplicate_rule, and upgrade validation should all reference the same rarity_tier map.

example_games.title: Diablo
example_games.note: Uses item rarity tiers as a central loot signal.

example_games.title: Borderlands
example_games.note: Uses rarity color and item generation as part of loot progression.
